source_jobs.title: Unexpected Awesome Person (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS 🍋

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.

As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: making investing in capital markets more accessible. By combining dwpbank’s long-standing expertise and stability with lemon.markets http://lemon.markets’ technology-first mindset and speed, we are building a modern, modular securities services ecosystem. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.




Your Mission

The title of this Job puts it very well. This is not about a specific role, but about you as a person, the whole package! Even though we are only actively looking for people to join us in Engineering, Operations and Brokerage right now, this does not mean that we don't need other roles to assemble a diverse & awesome team to change the brokerage industry.

In the next months, we will be looking for more lemoneers to join us, so we are more than happy to get to know you as early as possible and see if we can bring you in. It does not matter if you are experienced in Marketing, Engineering, Operations, Business Intelligence and Brokerage and want to support us with your unique experience. We would love to hear from you! 🍋



Who are you?

 - You are passionate about growing investing opportunities. Our mission resonates with you and you are excited to enable more companies to offer investment products, embed it into their product or bring investing into a new context.

 - You have a special power that brings lemon.markets to the next level. This does not mean you can fly or turn invisible, but that you bring along valuable experiences or a huge passion for brokerage.

 - You have no problem with learning new technology on the go. You will probably end up solving problems we did not think through, yet. This might require you to self-learn and grow while being able to produce output in the meantime.

 - You have been part of a (early-stage) startup or a similar lean organization before. Working in an early-stage startup can be fast-paced, challenging, and amazing at the same time. We want you to have experienced this before.

 - You enjoy building a product from scratch. Regardless, if you will be part of the product team or not, your role will be tied to the technology we built.

 - You always keep the bigger picture in mind. The tasks you do will only cover a small part of the whole company, we need you to be able to reflect on what you do and why you do it. 


How we take care of you: 

 - 🚀 Compensation package: We offer you a competitive salary, based on your experience and the value-add you bring to http://lemon.markets/lemon.markets.

 - 🕑 Flexible working hours: We trust people to choose when they work best and avoid a rigid 9 to 5 mindset. You decide on the rhythm that helps you do your best work, with only shared core hours from 12:00 to 16:00 to support collaboration.

 - 🏢 Hybrid work setup: You can work from anywhere in Germany within a hybrid model. At the same time, in-person collaboration is a key part of our culture. That being said, individual contributer lemoneers work 4 days or more & leadership lemoneers work 7 days or more per month on-site in our Berlin office, spending quality time together with the team at events, onsites/offsites or over team dinners.

source_jobs.title: Founder's Associate Intern (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS 🍋

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.

As part of the dwpbank group, we’re adding a layer of resources and expertise to benefit from, while continuing to operate as an independent fintech with our own team, product, and start-up identity under the dwpbank umbrella.



YOUR MISSION:

As a Founder’s Associate Intern, you will support the leadership team here at lemon.markets, making sure the business grows & runs smoothly, ensuring alignment across all business areas.

You will work on a selection of diverse projects ranging from Strategy & Operations, to Business Development & Go-to-Market. These projects are hard to forecast and will highly depend on needs evolving from the phase lemon.markets is in, as well as hugely on your interests and the skill set you already bring along.

You’ll get the best of both worlds: Experience first-hand how an early-stage start-up is being built, enjoy & benefit from a fast-paced and flexible environment, while working closely with experienced experts in their field. That means you will not only support and leverage your skill set, but learn & grow by taking on projects with a high degree of ownership.




YOU WILL:

 - Support the leadership team on strategic projects: Help develop business strategies & execute on them, making sure we stay aligned with our company goals & objectives.

 - Further, define & develop our GTM strategy: Continuously research and analyse market trends, conduct competitor activities, and stay up to date with the changing Brokerage landscape in Europe.

 - Drive various strategic initiatives to help us prepare for our growth post launch, ramping up business operations and ensuring operational excellence.

 - Critically question, analyse and improve existing processes. Constantly aim to improve our existing setup, build world-class processes around it, that enable us to have a smoothly running organisation.


WHO ARE YOU:

 - You have a background in Business Administration, Economics, Finance, Computer science or a similar area.

 - You are an entrepreneurial problem solver. You strive to take ownership of the tasks you get and apply your analytical skills, structured thinking, and independent working style towards solving them.

 - You have gained hands-on experience in a fast-paced environment. You understand what working in a startup or a similar lean organisation is like. Ideally, you have worked in an early-stage startup, fintech, consulting, tech company or similar.

 - You are ready and willing to take ownership. “This is not my job!” is not part of your vocabulary - you identify problems, take initiative to take them on, and challenge the status quo.

 - You cope well with frequent change and ambiguity. Changing tasks & requirements excite you. You are able to think logically, break down complex problems and prioritise between multiple tasks on your table.

 - You have a hands-on mentality. You default into action, want to gather first learnings fast, and iterate on the progress along the way.

 - You are a team player. Working together as a team and sharing knowledge comes naturally to you.

 - You have no problem with learning new tools and skills on the go. You try to solve problems with more things than the ones you already know. We will always enable you with that, but your willingness has to come first.



How we take care of you:

 - 🚀 Compensation packa

source_jobs.title: (Senior) Backend Engineer (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS 🍋

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.

As part of the dwpbank group, we’re adding a layer of resources and expertise to benefit from, while continuing to operate as an independent fintech with our own team, product, and start-up identity under the dwpbank umbrella.




YOUR MISSION:

As a Backend Engineer at lemon.markets you will play a key role in designing the backend services and architecture of our brokerage API platform that allows other companies to easily embed investing into their product. You will take ownership of features and services end to end in cross-functional teams, ensuring they run smoothly on production.

You will focus on areas of the Customer Lifecycle, Investing or Banking processes - making sure our Core Platform & Systems around it are reliable & scalable.

Our Tech Stack is Python, FastAPI, AWS and Postgres. While you can have worked in different modern tech stacks prior, you should be excited to work in Python and learn it if you haven’t worked with it before.

You will have a significant impact on the development of our core Platform & Systems - and thereby architectural decisions, as well as on the business itself. Work alongside a cross-functional team of experts, and together we will leverage your previous experiences to build the investment infrastructure for the next 100 million retail investors in Europe.




ABOUT YOU:

 - You have the ability to write well-structured and tested code in more than one high-level programming languages like Python, Golang, Java or Ruby, plus another language such as Javascript or Typescript.

 - You have experience in designing and implementing highly scalable architecture and reliable REST APIs and bring the desire to make them world-class.

 - You’ve gained good experience with software in a cloud environment (AWS, GCP, Azure) and the architectural patterns for this environment.

 - You have a good understanding of more than one document-based or relational database technology such as MySQL/PostgreSQL or MongoDB

 - You know how to write high-quality code that is understandable, maintainable and well tested, and can guide other engineers to do the same via code reviews.

 - You have good problem-solving skills and the ability to provide in-depth evaluations and analyses of unique and complex technological issues.

 - You have a track record of designing, building and operating reliable and scalable backend systems as part of a cross-functional team.
   
   


YOU WILL BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE IF:

 - Growing investment opportunities excites you. We operate in a highly regulated environment and therefore having worked prior in a Fintech or financial Service provider would give you a head start, but is not a must.

 - You see yourself as a product engineer. That means, you are excited by solving customer problems and strive to become an expert in the domain area you are working in.

 - You enjoy building from scratch. This means high involvement in the product development process itself while advocating for the best engineering practices. It also means that you enjoy taking technical decisions.

 - You are ready and willing to take ownership of problems and act on them decisively with no supervision. You always thrive for high quality outcomes through focus, determination and attention to detail.

 - You are a team player. Working together as a team, sharing

source_jobs.title: Senior Partner Manager (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS 🍋

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.

As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: making investing in capital markets more accessible. By combining dwpbank’s long-standing expertise and stability with lemon.markets’ technology-first mindset and speed, we are building a modern, modular securities services ecosystem. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.




YOUR MISSION:

At lemon.markets, you’ll own and grow partner/customer relationships end-to-end, from onboarding to launch, day-to-day operations, and expansion. As our Senior Partner Manager, you’re the partner’s main point of contact and their strongest internal advocate, orchestrating cross-functional teams to help partners build successful investment products on top of lemon.markets.

Our partner universe is currently evolving: alongside our direct business, where we typically work with fintechs and neobanks, we were recently acquired by dwpbank and are now building a combined offering with them. This means we’ll be welcoming a new wave of partners, with a strong focus on German banking partners, who will onboard through the joint motion and be supported by Partner Management from day one.

Your role is to make this journey feel customer-centric, structured, and straightforward, while ensuring we meet the regulatory and operational standards required to scale safely and sustainably.




IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL:

 - Support partner onboarding: While the Launch Manager leads the end-to-end onboarding from handover/intake to go-live, you’ll be involved from day one—partnering closely with Launch Management and internal teams to align on timelines, unblock topics, and ensure a smooth, successful launch.

 - Lead day-to-day partner management: Act as the single point of contact post-launch, run partner cadences, manage escalations, and represent partner needs internally.

 - Ensure regulatory & outsourcing readiness: Drive third-party risk, compliance, and outsourcing requirements end-to-end and help define/manage SLAs where relevant.

 - Drive partner growth: Identify expansion opportunities, translate them into concrete initiatives, and validate business cases to grow partner value and revenue.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally to improve product-market fit: Partner closely with Go-to-Market, Product, Brokerage, and Operations to feed insights back into our product and services.

 - Build and scale the Partner Management function: Develop frameworks, playbooks, and standards, and document learnings to continuously improve how we work.




YOU WILL BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE IF YOU:

 - Bring extensive experience in B2B Partner Management, Onboarding, or Project/Program Management, and you’re used to taking clear ownership of cross-functional delivery, driving outcomes beyond your own team.

 - Have experience in the financial industry, ideally in a brokerage, brokerage-related environment, fintech, or banking context and you can quickly build a strong understanding of our business.

 - Have experience managing a diverse partner portfolio across fast-moving fintechs/neobanks and (larger) German banks/corporate banks, adjusting your style to different decision-making cultures and stakeholder landscapes.

 - Have

source_jobs.title: Solutions Engineer (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS 🍋

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.

As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: we enable anyone to be an investor by making investing in capital markets more accessible. By combining dwpbank’s long-standing expertise and stability with lemon.markets’ technology-first mindset and speed, we are building a modern, modular securities services ecosystem. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.




YOUR MISSION:

As a Solutions Architect, you will be a pivotal force in driving the success of our commercial partnerships. You'll act as the crucial technical liaison, bridging the gap between our external partners and internal product development teams - ensuring that the solutions we shape with prospects are both commercially compelling and operationally deliverable. You help prospects understand how their investment use case maps to the lemon.markets platform and what it takes across teams to bring that use case to life.

This role offers a unique opportunity to influence the entire partner and product lifecycle, from supporting pre-sales go-to-market initiatives to ensuring seamless partner onboarding post-sales. You guide prospects from post‑discovery into deep qualification, uniting internal teams around a shared understanding of requirements, risks and solution paths. You turn complexity into clarity - structuring workshops, mapping operating models and coordinating across disciplines to make sure we design solutions that work end‑to‑end.

You are a strategic thinker, a problem solver, and a passionate advocate for both our partners and our product. You thrive in a dynamic, collaborative environment and are driven by the challenge of building lasting partnerships that drive innovation and growth. This is not a pure technical architecture role. It’s a highly cross‑functional, business‑aware role where you connect the dots across departments, shape how we present our product, and ensure we deliver comprehensive solutions - ultimately enabling Europe’s top FinTechs, Banks and Wealth Managers to build modern investment experiences.




YOU WILL:

 - Act as the cross‑functional bridge between Product, Engineering, Operations and GTM - ensuring all teams are aligned on the prospective partners needs and solution direction.

 - Cross-collaborate across teams to validate feasibility, clarify requirements and shape the best solution forward.

 - Work closely with GTM to win prospects’ trust in our technical solutions and win deals. This will comprise of the following:
   
   - Serve as the primary technical point of contact for prospective partners, and own the first technical impression, articulating the value proposition of our platform and demonstrating its capabilities
   
   - Conduct thorough technical discovery sessions to understand partner requirements and design tailored integration solutions, mapping the partner needs to our platform capabilities
   
   - Contribute to the creation of technical proposals as part of RfIs/RfPs, accurately reflecting partner requirements and connecting into our current and planned product capabilities
   
   - Stay abreast of industry trends and competitive landscape, providing technical insights to support sales and marketing effo

source_jobs.title: Talent & People Partner (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS 🍋

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.

As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: we enable anyone to be an investor by making investing in capital markets more accessible. By combining dwpbank’s long-standing expertise and stability with lemon.markets http://lemon.markets’ technology-first mindset and speed, we are building a modern, modular securities services ecosystem. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.




YOUR MISSION

As a Talent & People Partner, you’ll join our Talent Acquisition & People team during a key growth phase. Your focus will initially be on end-to-end hiring across both Business and Tech roles, helping us scale teams thoughtfully while keeping the bar high on quality of hire and candidate experience.

Working alongside Karo and Melissa, our two other Talent Partners, you’ll take direct ownership of roles and hiring processes, from aligning with hiring managers on the right profiles, to building strong pipelines, to running structured interview processes and guiding teams toward clear, timely decisions.

At the same time, this role goes beyond recruiting. We’re looking for someone who is excited to contribute across a broader People scope as well — from HR data and reporting to tooling and process optimisation, as well as people experience topics such as onboarding and onsite planning. As hiring needs evolve over time, this role will gradually take on more ownership across People Operations and analytics-related initiatives.

We’re looking for someone who will be hands-on in the day-to-day while also improving how we hire and how we operate as we scale, making sure our processes are efficient, consistent, data-informed, and focused on creating a strong experience for both candidates and employees.




IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL:

 - Lead 360° hiring across Business and Tech: Own end-to-end recruiting across multiple seniority levels, from role kick-off and profile definition with hiring managers, through screening and interviews, to offer management and negotiation.

 - Build and run effective sourcing strategies: Develop and execute sourcing approaches across channels like LinkedIn, GitHub, and Google to attract and engage talent across Operations, Commercial, Compliance, Product, and Tech.

 - Manage candidate relationships and process end-to-end: Build and maintain strong candidate pipelines, engage people with thoughtful outreach, and ensure a clear, respectful, and well-run experience throughout every stage.

 - Partner closely with hiring managers and interview teams: Translate team needs into strong hiring plans, keep stakeholders aligned, and help drive structured, timely decision-making.

 - Support People Operations and analytics topics: Take ownership of recurring HR reporting and KPI tracking, support initiatives such as performance review reporting, and help prepare monthly or quarterly people data insights.

 - Improve processes and tooling: Help optimise and automate workflows across tools such as Ashby and Personio, improving efficiency, consistency, and the quality of our people processes.

 - Contribute to people experience initiatives: Support and gradually take on ownership of topics such as onboarding, events, and onsite pl

source_jobs.title: Working Student - People (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS🍋

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses, providing seamless access to capital markets. By empowering FinTechs, Banks and Wealth Managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

lemon.markets provides a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform for simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure makes launching an investment product customer-centric and compliant. We abstract all complexity for our partners through a well documented API, a self-service customer operations portal and operating in a clear regulatory framework. Enabling partners to launch in weeks, not months.

We have experienced first hand that brokerage infrastructure is broken. Building an investment product used to be highly expensive, challenging and cumbersome. We ease this pain by shielding our partners & their customers from the underlying complexity - and with that, we make investing seamlessly accessible to everyone. Because that’s exactly what we enjoy, going to places nobody else dares and solving hard problems. This not only creates tons of value when done right, but attracts the smartest minds - because outlier companies are built by exceptional people like you.




YOUR MISSION:

As our People Working Student, you will help us create smooth and engaging employee experiences across the full employee lifecycle. From onboarding and payroll support to leading small projects and improving processes, you’ll keep our People processes running reliably while driving new ideas forward. You’ll also get the chance to explore talent sourcing and work with automation and AI to help us scale.

Who are you:

You are based in Berlin. You bring curiosity, structure, and communication skills to the table, and you’re motivated to build your career in People & Talent, no matter your study background. Experience with tools like Google Workspace, Slack, Personio, and sourcing platforms is a plus, but more important is your drive to learn and your passion for creating great employee experiences.




YOU WILL BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE IF:

 - You are structured, reliable and able to work independently while knowing when to ask for support.

 - You enjoy working with data, spreadsheets and digital tools and see automation and AI as opportunities to make work smarter.

 - You are a strong communicator who thrives in a collaborative environment and enjoys bringing people together (whether for onboarding, projects or events).

 - You’re curious, adaptable and motivated to grow your knowledge in our international People & Talent, from operational excellence to recruiting insights.

 - You are currently enrolled within a psychology, tourism, business administration, HR-related degree program.

 - You are comfortable writing and speaking in German and English and bring experience in working with G-Suite, and bring curiosity for AI tools.


HOW WE TAKE CARE OF YOU:

 - 🚀 Compensation package: Our working student positions are compensated at a fixed rate of €15 per hour.

 - 💎 Make a real impact: Our start-up environment gives you the room to start your career, explore areas outside your role, and build expertise through ownership. As we mature and are backed by dwpbank, we’re adding a layer of resources and expertise you are benefiting from.

 - 🕑 Flexible working hours: We know your studies come first. That’s why we offer flexible hours that adapt to your class schedule and exam periods, so you can plan your work time around your semester.

 - 🏢 Hybrid work setup: You can work from Berlin within a hybrid model. That being said, we encourage our working student lemoneers to come to the office regularly. Being on-site allows you to learn faster, provides a more immersive experience, helps you collaborate more effectively with the team, and ensures you fully benefit from hands-on support.

 - 📈 G

source_jobs.title: Banking Operations Specialist (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS 🍋

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.

As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: we enable anyone to be an investor by making investing in capital markets more accessible. By combining dwpbank’s long-standing expertise and stability with lemon.markets’ technology-first mindset and speed, we are building a modern, modular securities services ecosystem. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.




YOUR MISSION

As our Banking Operations Specialist, you’ll own the daily “money movement” engine end to end and help us build the function from the ground up. You’ll run payment cycles, reconcile bank activity every day, and ensure every bank transaction lands correctly in the ledger using clean double-entry logic.

When something doesn’t match, you’ll bring calm structure: investigate quickly, document what you did and why, resolve the root cause, and put guardrails in place so the same issue is less likely to happen again. You’ll also partner closely with Product and Engineering to improve tooling and automation, so the operation becomes more reliable, easier to run, and progressively less manual.

As the company grows, this role is designed to expand in scope. If we build a larger operations team over time, you’ll have the opportunity to step into leadership responsibilities, shaping processes, hiring/training new team members, and setting the operational standard for how banking ops runs at scale.




IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL:

 - Execute daily payment runs: you prepare payments, perform checks/controls, and manage release/approval steps.

 - Keep payment routing correct end-to-end: you ensure the right rails/providers/bank accounts are used and flows are handled cleanly.

 - Resolve payment exceptions: you monitor statuses and work through rejects/returns/failed payments with clear tracking and follow-through.

 - Run daily bank reconciliations: you reconcile bank statements vs. internal systems and ledger postings; investigate breaks and drive root-cause fixes.

 - Ensure ledger-accurate double-entry postings: you perform/validate bank-related bookings (clearing accounts, cut-offs, balancing logic; accruals where relevant) and keep them traceable.

 - Improve processes and controls: you strengthen SOPs, checklists, evidence standards, and handovers to reduce risk and manual work.

 - Partner with Product/Engineering on tooling: you turn operational needs into improvements (reporting, reconciliation logic, payment tooling, alerting/monitoring).




YOU’LL BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE IF YOU HAVE:

 - Relevant ops experience: you have 5+ years in Banking Ops, Payments Ops, Finance/Accounting Ops, Treasury Ops, or similar, ideally within a regulated environment.

 - Payments execution experience: you’ve run payment operations end-to-end, including exception handling (rejects/returns/failed payments).

 - Reconciliation and investigation skills: you are hands-on when it comes to daily reconciliations, structured break analysis, and thoughtful root-cause resolution.

 - Strong accounting and ledger foundation: you are confident with double-entry applied to bank flows, clearing accounts, balancing logic, and cut-offs.

 - High accuracy and audit-ready documentation: you’re det

source_jobs.title: (Senior) Corporate Actions Specialist (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS 🍋

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.

As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: making investing in capital markets more accessible. By combining dwpbank’s long-standing expertise and stability with lemon.markets http://lemon.markets’ technology-first mindset and speed, we are building a modern, modular securities services ecosystem. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.



Your Mission

As lemon.markets continues to grow and expands the range of instruments on our platform, corporate actions will naturally become a bigger and more important part of our day-to-day operations. That’s why we’re hiring our first Corporate Actions Specialist: someone who takes full ownership of the end-to-end lifecycle, from announcement capture and validation through elections, entitlement calculation, to final settlement and reconciliation. You’ll work closely with Product and Engineering to shape how the process should run and to translate it into clear documentation and system requirements. Once live, you’ll ensure the daily execution is smooth, consistent, and customer-first, because these events sit at the heart of a reliable investing experience. Over time, you’ll help us automate as much as possible as volumes grow, while also raising corporate actions knowledge across the Investing Operations team. This role is a unique chance to build, run, and continuously optimise an entire function.



In this role you will:

 - Own the end-to-end corporate actions lifecycle across our growing instrument universe, from announcement capture and validation through elections processing, entitlement calculation, and final settlement.

 - Partner with Product and Engineering to design and scale the operating model, turning real-world event flows into clear requirements, workflows, and screens that work in practice.

 - Coordinate seamlessly with external stakeholders (custodians, issuers, depositories, brokers, and other counterparties) to ensure events are processed accurately and on time.

 - Manage regulatory, tax, and market reporting obligations, meeting disclosure requirements, market deadlines, and internal control standards.

 - Drive continuous improvement and automation, standardising processes, reducing operational risk, and implementing system and tooling enhancements as volumes increase.

 - Build and share corporate actions knowledge internally, creating SOPs, event playbooks, operational handbooks, and policy documentation to upskill the broader team.
   
   

You’ll be successful in this role if you:

 - Bring deep, hands-on experience in Corporate Actions Operations across fintechs, banks, custodians, brokers, or asset servicers, covering the full end-to-end lifecycle from event capture and validation through elections, taxation, reconciliation, and settlement. Exposure to broader investment/market operations is a strong plus.

 - Have practical familiarity with the German retail investing market, and understand how European regulatory requirements translate into day-to-day operational realities (e.g., tax reporting, disclosures, and market deadlines).

 - Are comfortable operating within custody structures and market infrastructure (including CSDs/depositories) and can b

source_jobs.title: Customer Operations Lead (f/m/d)

As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: making investing in capital markets more accessible. By combining dwpbank’s long-standing expertise and stability with lemon.markets http://lemon.markets’ technology-first mindset and speed, we are building a modern, modular securities services ecosystem. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.



Your Mission

You will ensure lemon.markets delivers a fast, reliable, and compliant customer lifecycle so our partners can launch and scale investment products with confidence. Sitting in the 1st Line of Defense (1LoD), you’ll lead Customer Ops, KYC Ops, and AFC Ops and make sure day-to-day operations run smoothly while meeting regulatory expectations. You’ll report to our Head of Operations and work closely with Banking Ops and Securities Ops, plus key stakeholders in our Customer Lifecycle domain (Product & Engineering) and our 2nd Line of Defense (Compliance/Risk). Over the first months, your focus will be to bring a fresh, data-driven perspective, quickly diagnosing the biggest drivers of volume, risk, and customer pain. You’ll build a clear prioritization roadmap and execute it. Your main mission is to turn the department into a highly automated machine by redesigning workflows and automating as much as possible (while keeping compliance guardrails intact).

In this role you will:

 - Develop and lead a team of FTEs and working students across Customer Ops, KYC Ops, and AFC Ops, setting clear expectations, coaching, and building a high-ownership culture.

 - Run and improve day-to-day performance across the lifecycle by steering SLAs, backlogs, cycle times, quality, and rework, ensuring customers and partners get predictable, reliable outcomes.

 - Establish an operating system for the area (daily/weekly reviews, capacity planning, queue/skill management) that makes priorities, bottlenecks, and decisions transparent.

 - Own and evolve KYC operations (onboarding, verification, document checks, remediation, periodic reviews where relevant) with strong QA standards, sampling, and root-cause work to reduce KYC aging and risk.

 - Own AFC case & alert operations including evidence collection, escalation logic, and audit trails to ensure defensible decisions and strong audit readiness.

 - Partner with Product, Engineering, and 2LoD to translate regulatory and operational needs into scalable workflows, permissions, logging, and controls and ensure every change is operationally ready (SOPs, training, rollout/support).

 - Lead automation initiatives across the whole Ops function by eliminating manual work through workflow automation, decision engines, etc. driving efficiency, consistency, and faster turnaround times while reducing operational risk.

 - Own vendor management and communication, ensuring external partners/tools meet our requirements for performance, scalability, and auditability.



You’ll be successful in this role if you:

 - Bring multi-year operations experience in a regulated environment (FinTech/Banking) having hands-on expertise across Customer Service Ops, KYC Ops, and AFC Ops. Brokerage experience is a strong plus.

 - Have proven people leadership experience (coachi

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS 🍋

We grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses, providing seamless access to capital markets. We enable everyone to be an investor.

lemon.markets builds infrastructure for financial services and technology companies powering the next generation of investment products. From enabling a FinTech to launch their first investment product, over allowing e.g. a Neobank to embed investing into their product to bringing investing into a completely new context. Our API-first infrastructure makes launching an investment product customer-centric and compliant. We abstract all complexity for our partners through a well documented API, a self-service customer operations portal and operating in a clear regulatory framework. Enabling partners to launch in weeks, not months.

We have experienced first hand that brokerage infrastructure is broken. Building an investment product used to be highly expensive, challenging and cumbersome. We ease this pain by shielding our partners & their customers from the underlying complexity - and with that we make investing more accessible to everyone. Because that’s exactly what we enjoy, going to places nobody else dares and solving hard problems. This not only creates tons of value when done right, but attracts the smartest minds - because outlier companies are built by exceptional (& diverse) people like you.




YOUR MISSION:

As Principal Product Manager (Enterprise), your core mission is to establish the product direction and strategic direction for our Enterprise product which is powered by the joint capabilities of lemon.markets and dwpbank. This is a high-impact, newly created role that sits across product strategy, product management and cross-organisational collaboration and drives opportunities from concept to launch and adoption.

Reporting to the Head of Product, you will drive work autonomously with deep ownership, collaborating with the leadership team at lemon.markets and dwpbank, with our product organisation and our cross-functional domains and broader functions. This is a unique opportunity to shape our enterprise strategy, delivering a direct and measurable impact on our future growth.




YOU WILL:

 - Shape and drive forward our Enterprise product by identifying, evaluating and prioritising customer needs through a deep understanding of the market, our organisational context, our existing capabilities and the new capabilities we need.

 - Take a critical view of current product opportunities, group opportunities and propose a clear roadmap and influence both lemon.markets and dwpbank to deliver it

 - Act as the primary product interface between lemon.markets and dwpbank for Enterprise opportunities, managing stakeholders at all levels including senior leadership.

 - Lead by example, demonstrating strong product craft across the product development lifecycle by providing clear product direction, clarifying scope, making key priority calls, distilling alignment and creating clarity on new product and partner launches.

 - Work cross-function and cross-domain across engineering, operations, compliance, risk and business development to scope solutions and ensure execution.

 - Support org-wide craft standards and build scalable frameworks for how Enterprise product work is structured, documented and measured.

 - Manage and develop your direct report, fostering their growth and ensuring high-quality delivery.

 - Anticipate market and regulatory shifts that affect Enterprise opportunities and proactively adapt the strategy.

 - Drive decision-making with data, continuously forming hypotheses, running experiments and measuring impact.

 - Represent the product function externally with partners, vendors and enterprise clients where appropriate.
   
   


YOU WILL BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE IF:

 - You bring 7+ years of product management experience, with a strong track record building and scaling complex, mission-critical platforms in financ

As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: we enable anyone to be an investor by making investing in capital markets more accessible. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.




YOUR MISSION

As our Market Operations Specialist, you will help ensure that our daily trading and post-trade operations run reliably, transparently, and at scale. You will join the Investing Operations team, working closely with colleagues across investment operations, corporate actions, and securities operations, and collaborate with Product, Engineering, Compliance, and external partners where operational processes intersect. Your work will directly support smooth order handling, robust controls, regulatory compliance, and a high-quality operational backbone for our partners and their end customers. Over the next 6 to 12 months, your focus will be on executing and stabilising recurring market operations processes, improving exception handling, and helping bring more structure and transparency to a growing operational setup. The role sits at the intersection of trading operations, process discipline, and cross-functional problem-solving in a regulated environment. This creates the opportunity to improve reliability and scalability while reducing manual effort, operational risk, and friction across counterparties and internal systems.




IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL:

 - Execute recurring market operations and trading-related processes across the order and post-trade lifecycle, ensuring reliable daily operations and consistent service quality for partners and end investors.

 - Monitor core controls such as best execution oversight, regulatory reporting, and instrument master data management, helping maintain operational accuracy, compliance, and transparency.

 - Investigate operational exceptions, breaks, and failed workflows together with internal stakeholders and external partners, resolving issues quickly to reduce risk and avoid downstream disruption.

 - Collaborate with Product and Engineering to surface tooling gaps, process pain points, and automation opportunities, enabling more scalable workflows and less manual operational effort.

 - Support reconciliations, reporting, and issue resolution across systems and counterparties, helping create a stronger control environment and more reliable operational outcomes.

 - Improve documentation, process visibility, and operational discipline across recurring and one-off workflows, so the team can scale sustainably and respond effectively in a dynamic market environment.




YOU’LL BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE IF YOU:

 - You bring hands-on experience from market operations, brokerage operations, middle or back office, trade support, or a similar environment with exposure to pre-trade, trading, and post-trade processes.

 - You have broad knowledge of financial instruments, market infrastructure, and the operational realities of working with banks, brokers, exchanges, market makers, or fintechs.

 - You are confident with reco

YOUR MISSION

As our Launch Manager, you’ll own the end-to-end delivery of our partner onboarding, from kick-off through go-live and post-launch activities, turning complex, regulated brokerage integrations into predictable, high-quality launches.

Our partner universe is evolving: alongside our direct business with fintechs and neobanks, we’re building a combined offering with dwpbank, bringing a new wave of partners and increased cross-organization complexity. We’re therefore building a dedicated Launch Manager function to run these onboardings with clear ownership.

In this setup, you’ll provide the coordination we’re building, driving the launch plan, steering stakeholders, and ensuring a clean handover to Partner Management once the partner is live. You’ll sit at the center of cross-functional execution, aligning Partnerships, Product, Engineering, Operations, and second-line functions (and, where relevant, dwpbank) to keep delivery on time, within scope, and operationally sound.




IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL:

 - Lead end-to-end partner launches by owning the onboarding plan, milestones, and delivery rhythm across Partnerships, Product, Engineering, Operations, and second-line functions — so partners go live faster and with fewer surprises.

 - Drive alignment and decision-making between partnership leads, product counterparts, and executive sponsors — so scope, priorities, and trade-offs are clear and execution stays on track.

 - Run cross-functional workstreams (e.g., API integration readiness, ops/process setup, compliance & controls, partner enablement) to reduce delivery risk and ensure high-quality outcomes in a regulated environment.

 - Design and continuously improve an onboarding playbook (templates, checklists, risk log, status reporting, launch criteria) to standardize onboarding and scale partner delivery without scaling chaos.

 - Own stakeholder communication and reporting to leadership, internal steering committees, and partner counterparts — so progress, risks, and dependencies are transparent and manageable.

 - Manage complex/time-sensitive post-launch activities (feature expansions, new functionalities, operational changes) so existing partners can grow their offering without destabilizing day-to-day operations.




YOU’LL BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE IF:

 - You bring 5+ years of experience in launch management or project/program management within brokerage, fintech, or banking environments.

 - You have a deep understanding of B2B onboarding, including how to structure plans, manage dependencies, and steer stakeholders through complex delivery.

 - You bring strong financial services domain expertise (brokerage knowledge is a plus) and can translate regulatory/operational constraints into actionable delivery plans.

 - You have led complex cross-fun

lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.




YOUR MISSION

As our KYC Operations Specialist, you will help ensure that our B2B client onboarding and lifecycle processes are compliant, reliable, and scalable in a regulated environment. You will report to the Customer Operations Lead and work in the 1LoD alongside AFC Operations and Customer Service, collaborating closely with the Customer Lifecycle Domain, 2LoD Compliance, and KYC teams at our partners. Your work will directly support smooth client onboarding, strong risk management, and accurate customer account data, helping us follow best practices while enabling our partners to operate with confidence. Over the next 6 to 12 months, your focus will be on executing high-quality end-to-end KYC processes, strengthening routine controls, and helping improve the operational setup around customer lifecycle and compliance-related workflows. The role sits at the intersection of operations, compliance, and partner collaboration, where good judgment and attention to detail have a direct impact on risk, audit readiness, and customer experience. This is a strong opportunity to shape and improve mission-critical processes as they scale in complexity and volume.




IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL:

 - Oversee end-to-end KYC processes for B2B clients together with internal stakeholders and partner KYC teams, including onboarding, periodic reviews, and offboarding, to ensure compliant and efficient customer lifecycle management.

 - Conduct due diligence on corporate clients by reviewing legal entity structures, UBOs, source of funds, and corporate documentation, enabling robust risk assessments and sound onboarding decisions.

 - Assess client risk profiles in line with internal policies and regulatory requirements, escalating high-risk clients, suspicious activity, and compliance concerns to AFC Operations, Compliance, and other relevant teams when needed.

 - Maintain accurate customer records, KYC documentation, and risk assessments across internal systems, ensuring audit readiness, timely renewals of key documents, and reliable customer account data.

 - Collaborate closely with the Customer Lifecycle Domain, AFC Operations, Customer Service, 2LoD Compliance, and external partners to resolve KYC-related issues, clarify requirements, and keep operational processes running smoothly.

 - Improve KYC workflows, work instructions, and reporting together with cross-functional teams, helping the organisation scale operations, adapt to regulatory changes, and reduce manual effort over time.
   
   


YOU’LL BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE IF YOU:

 - You bring 5+ years of relevant experience in Customer Operations or KYC Operations within the financial industry, ideally in a fintech or similarly fast-moving regulated environment.

 - You have a deep understanding of KYC, AML, and CTF requirements

You ensure onboarding, changes, and offboarding are executed end-to-end with high quality, clear communication, and audit-ready documentation in a regulated environment. You are the owner of our People Operations workflows and data in Personio, with a strong GDPR-first mindset.
You will spend most of your time keeping day-to-day People Operations running smoothly and improving the engine through automation, standardisation, and documentation. You will use dedicated capacity to drive project work based on business needs and operational insights you identify through daily operations.
You sit in the People team and partner closely with Talent, Finance, and Office, reporting into the People Business Partner.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Employee lifecycle + payroll ownership: Own end-to-end People Operations across onboarding, changes, and offboarding, plus the monthly payroll process. Partner with the People Business Partner, Finance, and our external tax advisor to ensure smooth execution and accurate payroll inputs to reduce operational risk and keep teams moving fast.

 - Employee support + request flow: Operate our People request flow via structured intake (e.g., ticketing/forms). Work with leaders and employees to prioritise and resolve cases with clear updates to strengthen employee support, improve the overall employee experience, and enable predictable delivery.

 - Personio administration & optimisation: Build and continuously improve our Personio setup (workflows, templates, permissions, data quality checks, reporting). Collaborate with the People Business Partner and key stakeholders so decisions are based on reliable People data and manual work decreases.

 - Compliance & data handling: Ensure compliance-by-design in People Operations. Align with Compliance, Legal, and Finance to keep documentation GDPR-first, access secure, and an audit trail in place to lower risk in a regulated environment.

 - Documentation & standardisation: Standardise and document People Operations processes in Confluence. Work with cross-functional stakeholders to create clear process docs, checklists, and templates to reduce dependency on tribal knowledge and increase consistency.

 - Automation & project improvements: Improve and automate recurring People Operations work through project-based initiatives. Partner with subject-matter experts and tool owners to remove bottlenecks and error-prone steps to increase reliability and create more capacity for growth.

source_jobs.title: (Senior) Legal Counsel (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS 🍋

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.

As part of the dwpbank group, we share a clear goal: we enable anyone to be an investor by making investing in capital markets more accessible. By combining dwpbank’s long-standing expertise and stability with lemon.markets’ technology-first mindset and speed, we are building a modern, modular securities services ecosystem. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.




YOUR MISSION:

At lemon.markets, we're building the infrastructure that powers the next generation of investment products. As we scale, and navigate an incoming wave of new regulations, we need a sharp, experienced legal mind to join our growing Legal team.

This is not a standard in-house legal role. We are a frontrunner in a fast-moving, largely uncharted space, and the legal work reflects that: highly creative, non-standard, and intellectually stimulating at every turn. If you are energised by the idea of helping define what the rulebook looks like rather than just following it, you'll feel right at home here.

You will work closely with our General Counsel and an experienced Legal team, taking on a high degree of personal ownership with strong visibility to senior management, including our Managing Directors and Founder.



You will:

 - Own the contract work. The majority of your time will be spent drafting contracts from scratch. Because lemon.markets operates in a non-standard space as an infrastructure provider, templates rarely exist. You'll be creating the language that makes novel investment products legally enforceable.

 - Review and negotiate agreements received from partners, clients and counterparties, and represent lemon.markets' interests with confidence in negotiations — including with German-speaking counterparties.

 - Enable product development. You act as a critical legal checkpoint for ideas coming from our Product and Sales teams. Your role is to translate innovative concepts into legally feasible and properly documented offerings, or flag where something simply can't be done.

 - Monitor regulatory developments particularly in securities law (Wertpapierrecht), capital markets law (Kapitalmarktrecht) and banking law (Bankrecht). Translate regulatory changes into concrete contract language and proactively communicate their impact across the organisation.

 - Support the General Counsel in shaping the company's legal strategy, including our European expansion.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with inter alia Compliance, AML, Data Protection, and other teams who will regularly seek your input.

 - Advise senior leadership. You'll have direct visibility to the Managing Directors and Founder, both as subject-matter expert and as a deputy to the General Counsel.
   
   

You will be successful in this role if:

 - You are a fully qualified German lawyer (Volljurist) with strong academic foundations and 3 to 8 years of post-qualification experience (PQE).

 - You have solid, hands-on expertise in securities law (Wertpapierrecht), capital markets law (Kapitalmarktrecht), and/or banking law (Bankrecht), ideally gained at a major law firm, Fintech or Banking environment.

 - You have strong foundations in contract law and are comfortable by the challenge

source_jobs.title: DevOps Engineer (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LEMON.MARKETS 🍋

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.

As part of the dwpbank group, we’re adding a layer of resources and expertise to benefit from, while continuing to operate as an independent fintech with our own team, product, and start-up identity under the dwpbank umbrella.




YOUR MISSION:

As a member of our cross-functional team of DevOps, DevSecOps and Workplace Technology engineers, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the direction and evolution of our infrastructure. You’ll build on a stable foundation that includes EKS on AWS, IaC principles, and robust deployment pipelines. Together, we’ll drive architectural enhancements, ensure compliance with regulatory standards, and support the growing demands of our business.

Your work will significantly impact both our internal engineering teams and the external partners who depend on secure, reliable infrastructure. Leveraging your expertise, you’ll help us continue to deliver scalable and compliant solutions that empower our partners to innovate and bring investment opportunities to millions across Europe.




ABOUT YOU:

 - You have hands-on experience with infrastructure-as-code tools such as Terraform or OpenTofu and are passionate about leveraging these to build reliable, scalable systems.

 - You have experience in AWS networking, core services (e.g., RDS, S3), and IAM security best practices.

 - You are skilled in managing Kubernetes (we use EKS) and are excited to maintain and evolve enterprise-scale platforms with best practices for security, privacy, monitoring, and CI/CD.

 - You have experience with observability tooling such as OpenTelemetry and are comfortable working with tools like DataDog to implement metrics, tracing, and robust monitoring solutions.

 - You bring a solid understanding of cloud-native technologies, including AWS services like RDS and RedShift, and know how to integrate them into highly performant and secure infrastructure.

 - You are familiar with modern CI/CD pipelines and workflow automation tools, including ArgoCD, Argo Workflows, and GitHub Actions, and you use them to streamline operations and improve developer productivity.

 - You have a proven ability to design, build, and maintain scalable platforms while working as part of a cross-functional team.


YOU WILL BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE IF:

 - You can dive into an existing codebase and quickly familiarize yourself, identifying areas to improve and contribute effectively.

 - You see yourself as a product engineer. That means you are excited by solving customer problems and strive to become an expert in the domain area you are working in.

 - You are a team player. Working together as a team, sharing knowledge, and providing mentorship comes naturally to you. Long term, you may even see yourself stepping into a team lead role.

 - You understand what working in a startup or a similar lean organization is like. You are comfortable working with a small team, moving quickly, and focusing on what truly matters.

 - You are ready and willing to take ownership of problems and act on them decisively with little supervision. You always aim for high-quality outcomes through focus, determination, and attention to detail.

 - You enjoy learning new technologies and thrive on solving challenges with tools and methods beyond those you already know.

We're looking for people with passion, desire to l

lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.




YOUR MISSION

As our Investment Compliance Manager, you will own and strengthen our Investment Compliance Function across the securities business, ensuring that our products, processes, and partner activities remain fully aligned with WPHG requirements. The role sits close to our Investing Domain and Customer Lifecycle Domain and is designed as an embedded compliance role with strong exposure to Product, Engineering, Operations, Partner Management, and other first-line functions. Your work will directly support compliant product growth by ensuring that new features, instruments, customer journeys, and partner setups are reviewed early and implemented in line with regulatory expectations. In the first months, your focus will be on understanding the existing framework, taking end-to-end ownership of day-to-day WPHG compliance topics, and further detailing and improving an already solid control setup. You will have the opportunity to combine oversight with forward-looking advisory work, helping the business move quickly while maintaining high regulatory standards in a product-driven and highly collaborative environment.




IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL:

 - Review key WPHG topics such as best execution, inducements, appropriateness, product governance, customer classification, conflicts of interest, market abuse, safeguarding of customer assets, and complaint-related processes, ensuring that the existing framework is applied correctly and further developed where needed.

 - Monitor first-line implementation and controls across customer order handling, partner activities, tied-agent setups, and customer-facing journeys, including marketing materials, app flows, and disclosures, to ensure customers are informed clearly and not steered inappropriately.

 - Advise Product, Engineering, Operations in the Investing and Customer Lifecycle Domains on new features, financial instruments, customer types, and process changes, so regulatory requirements are considered early and product development can move forward in a compliant way.

 - Collaborate closely with Partner Management and relevant internal teams to assess whether partner-operated materials and processes under our license meet our standards and regulatory obligations.

 - Prepare and further develop WPHG compliance reporting for management, including regular assessments of business development, customer and transaction volumes, incidents, and potential deviations from internal or regulatory requirements.
   
   


YOU’LL BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE IF YOU:

 - You bring at least 5 years of professional experience, including a minimum of 3 years of hands-on WPHG compliance experience in a licensed fintech or bank, with a clear focus on securities services or securities distribution.

 - You have strong expertise in German regulatory

source_jobs.title: VP People (f/m/d)

We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.

As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: we enable anyone to be an investor by making investing in capital markets more accessible. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market





YOUR MISSION

As lemon.markets enters its next stage of growth, we are looking for a VP People to help strengthen the company from the inside out.

This role goes beyond leading People & Talent. You will help us move from a setup that is already functioning reasonably well into a more mature, scalable, and consistent organization. Working closely with our CEO, leadership team, and key stakeholders across lemon.markets and dwpbank, you will strengthen the People function, build stronger leadership structures and rituals, shape culture intentionally, and improve collaboration across both organizations.

Reporting directly to our CEO and being part of our extended executive board, you will own the People & Talent function, moderate key leadership forums, and help build the structures, principles, and rhythms that a more mature organization needs.





IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL:

 - Own the People & Talent function end-to-end, lead the team, and continue to strengthen existing foundations across talent acquisition, compensation, performance, feedback, career development, and people policies

 - Embed and scale the processes, standards, and initiatives already in place, ensuring they are consistently lived in the day-to-day reality of lemon.markets
